question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Flux.fromStream() does not enforce backpressure on the underlying Java Stream from topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(), causing the entire result set to be eagerly loaded into memory regardless of downstream demand, and by opening many subscriptions simultaneously exhaust heap memory and trigger a JVM crash?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe logging (log.info) in listen() to cause log flooding by rapidly subscribing and unsubscribing, generating millions of log entries per second that fill disk storage, causing the mirror node to crash due to disk full errors and halting all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter where the computed startTime (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) equals Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the SQL query in findByFilter() to use WHERE consensus_timestamp >= Long.MAX_VALUE, which while returning no results still executes on every poll interval, and with thousands of such subscriptions creates a constant DB query load that prevents transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) combined with withFixedDelay(interval) to create a scenario where the Flux never terminates even after the subscriber disconnects (due to improper cancellation propagation), causing zombie polling tasks to accumulate on the scheduler and eventually exhaust all available threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timeout on topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() within poll() to cause indefinite blocking of a boundedElastic() scheduler thread by triggering a slow DB query (e.g., via a topic with billions of messages and an old startTime), and by opening enough such subscriptions fill all scheduler threads and halt the entire polling infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that PollingContext is created per-subscription (not shared) and each holds an AtomicReference<TopicMessage> that retains the last TopicMessage object in memory, and by subscribing to a topic with very large message payloads (e.g., maximum-size chunk messages) cause heap exhaustion across thousands of concurrent subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.toBuilder() call in poll() to trigger excessive object allocation (new TopicMessageFilter per poll cycle per subscription), and with thousands of subscriptions polling every 500ms generate millions of short-lived objects per second that overwhelm the GC and cause stop-the-world pauses that halt transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum subscriber count check in listen() to register Long.MAX_VALUE subscriptions (limited only by system resources), each independently consuming a DB connection and scheduler thread, causing a cascading resource exhaustion that prevents the network from confirming any new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DB connection pool deadlock by opening subscriptions that each hold a DB connection open via Flux.fromStream() while waiting for downstream demand that never comes (slow consumer), and simultaneously opening new subscriptions that require DB connections for their poll() calls, exhausting the pool and preventing any new connections including those needed for transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) to schedule a large number of deferred subscriptions that all activate simultaneously after the interval elapses, creating a synchronized thundering herd of poll() calls that overwhelms the database at a precise moment and causes a transaction confirmation outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) calculation by providing a limit value that when cast to int produces Integer.MAX_VALUE (due to overflow), causing Math.min to return maxPageSize=5000 normally, but if the cast produces a value between 0 and maxPageSize, causing undersized pages that never advance the cursor and create an infinite polling loop?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tag(METRIC_TAG, 'poll') metric tagging to cause a metric registry memory leak by subscribing with unique subscriber IDs (from TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId random generation), creating unbounded metric tag combinations that exhaust the ObservationRegistry heap allocation and crash the JVM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.fromStream() wrapping of a JDBC-backed stream to cause a connection leak when the subscriber cancels the subscription mid-stream (between poll cycles), leaving the underlying ResultSet and PreparedStatement open and eventually exhausting the DB connection pool, preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec jitter(0.1) randomization to cause non-deterministic timing that defeats any rate-limiting or circuit-breaker mechanisms, allowing a sustained polling attack that appears as normal traffic but continuously degrades DB performance until transaction confirmation fails?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of error handling in poll() (no onErrorResume or retry) to cause a single DB exception to propagate up through the Flux pipeline and terminate the entire subscription, but if this happens to a shared resource or causes a cascading error in the scheduler, it could halt all polling activity and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() does not validate the TopicMessageFilter before creating a PollingContext, allowing a filter with an invalid or null topicId to be passed to poll(), causing a NullPointerException in topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() that propagates and crashes the reactive pipeline, potentially affecting other subscribers sharing the same scheduler?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime computation (last != null ? last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 : filter.getStartTime()) by subscribing to a topic where the last message has consensusTimestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1, causing the +1 to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE and resetting the poll window to the beginning of time, generating a massive DB query that crashes the system?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the boundedElastic() scheduler's task queue (which is unbounded by default in some Reactor versions) by submitting thousands of subscriptions that each enqueue poll() tasks faster than they can be processed, causing the task queue to grow without bound and exhaust heap memory, crashing the JVM and halting all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a circuit breaker around topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() in poll() to cause a DB overload cascade: when the DB is slow, poll() tasks pile up on the scheduler, which causes more threads to block, which causes more tasks to queue, eventually exhausting all resources and preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnNext(context::onNext) callback to cause a race condition in PollingContext where count.incrementAndGet() and last.set() are not atomically updated together, allowing a concurrent poll() call to read an inconsistent state (old last but new count), causing the limit calculation to produce an incorrect pageSize that results in missed messages or infinite polling?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() pattern to trigger a validation failure in TopicMessageFilter (e.g., isValidStartTime() or isValidEndTime() returning false) when the computed startTime exceeds endTime, causing an exception that terminates the polling Flux and prevents further message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer() wrapping of poll(context) to cause repeated re-evaluation of the poll logic on each repeat cycle, and by manipulating the filter's startTime to always return the same timestamp, cause an infinite loop of identical DB queries that never advance state and permanently saturate the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum execution time limit on the Flux.fromStream() in poll() to cause a long-running DB query to hold a scheduler thread indefinitely, and by opening enough such subscriptions fill the boundedElastic() thread pool, preventing any new poll() tasks from executing and halting transaction message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() call in doOnSubscribe to cause a logging framework deadlock or synchronization bottleneck when thousands of subscriptions are created simultaneously, blocking the scheduler threads that are waiting to acquire the logging lock and preventing poll() tasks from executing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that poll() creates a new TopicMessageFilter via toBuilder() on every invocation (every 500ms per subscription), and with thousands of subscriptions generate millions of object allocations per second that trigger frequent GC cycles, causing stop-the-world pauses that halt the reactive event loop and prevent transaction confirmation?
